My longtime relationship with antiperspirants was like that of a social smoker and cigarettes: I knew they were ultimately harmful and comforted myself with the notion that I would one day just. get used to them. But here we are, 11 years into my relationship with Dove Men+Care, and the brand has been making an effort to convince me otherwise.

In 2007, the company released Dove Men+Care Shower Gel, a liquid deodorant that claimed to be "natural" without claiming to actually be natural. Soon thereafter, Dove Men+Care was launched in two other iterations, Dove Men+Care Fresh & Fresh Shower Gel and Dove Men+Care Fresh & Fresh Shower Oil, both of which promised that the product’s scent was not derived from ingredients added by the consumer. In 2008, Dove Men+Care was expanded to include the Shower Foam and the Shower Gels, both of which featured claims that the products were naturally scented.

Advertisement

The new formulations came with a catch: The products were to be sold only in stores that carried Dove Men+Care products, and the products themselves could not be purchased via the internet or the pharmacy.

Everyone talks about the technical talent crunch in Boston (and other hubs). What about the human resources crunch?

"The real challenge is finding people who can code," said Mark Bittman, co-founder and CEO of Stripe. "You can't hire for any skillset you want."

So companies are trying to find ways to develop people who are good at other things besides coding.

"Companies are putting together a lot more initiatives to develop their own product teams," said Jim Kelleher, co-founder of the company behind the well-known Slack app.

A recent study by staffing firm Challenger, Gray & Christmas found that 40 percent of employers in the U.S. were having trouble finding new hires with skills other than coding.

Bittman said Stripe has made hiring people with technical skills a key part of its recruiting strategy -- especially with the help of its new engineering team.

"At Stripe we've taken engineering out of our recruiting process," he said. "We have a very clear set of requirements for engineers.

CRAIG Joubert, the referee at the centre of the controversy over Scotland’s narrow Rugby World Cup quarter final loss to Australia last year, has revealed why he ran off the pitch at.

The match was decided by a four-point penalty in a tense battle at Twickenham that saw the Aussies outscore the Scots by 29-0.

The match was decided by a four-point penalty in a tense battle at Twickenham that saw the Aussies outscore the Scots by 29-0.

In an interview with Sky Sports News, Joubert revealed he wanted to leave the field of play after he had been sent off for a high tackle on Australia’s centre Andrew Kellaway.

Joubert was sent off for a high tackle (Reuters)

The referee felt that Joubert’s high tackle on Kellaway would have led to an Australian touchdown but Joubert argued that the tackle put him in danger of injury and he lost control of his body as he attempted to get back onto the pitch.

The referee sent Joubert off and said that he was not sure he could continue on the pitch, while the home side went on to win the game 36-31.

Dr. Rahul Gupta is the senior vice president and chief medical and health officer at March of Dimes.
If some state lawmakers have their way, pregnant women who misuse opioids or other drugs. will no longer be considered to be in need of those medications. The move could have a big impact on mothers who are addicted but still receiving prenatal care.
A bill before the House Public Health Committee would only allow these women to get opioids if they have a doctor's orders for them.
"We certainly don't want to take away the medications that are necessary," Dr. Rahul Gupta, the senior vice president and chief medical and health officer at March of Dimes, said. "I think there should be some kind of a grace period."
Gupta said he would like to see an increase in access to medication-assisted treatment for pregnant women who use drugs.
"We certainly believe that we should be supporting pregnant women who are suffering from drug addiction," he said. "And it does not seem to me that we have enough resources to provide that level of care."
The reason that women may be able to request opioids when they don't have a prescription is that the Legislature has allowed more people to receive prescriptions for opioid painkillers.

It's easy to dump shortcuts, files and other miscellany on to your desktop for quick access or for convenience's sake, but before long you can be left with a forest of icons. If you ever get the urge to delete them, the only way to do it is from the command line!

A new tool called IconCleaner will help you to do just that with a simple command that allows you to delete all the icons in your desktop, folder or application. It's also possible to remove icons by application or window.

To use it, simply type IconCleaner at the command prompt:

iconcleaner

It will tell you if it's working correctly and then run the icon cleaner program. When done, it'll return with a list of all the icons in your desktop, folder or application.

Nicola Sturgeon has assured the family of Sheku Bayoh, the Sierra Leonean man who died on a pavement in Kirkcaldy, Fife, minutes after being detained by police in May, that she will.

The First Minister said she had already spoken to Mrs Bayoh and her family to express her sympathies following the death of Sheku, and that she would make sure the family were properly cared for.

She added: "It is right that we are going to make sure that we support the family in any way that we can. It is important that we do that."

She also confirmed that the Fife Police Service had been asked to conduct an independent review into the incident.

Police Scotland have already said they were reviewing the circumstances surrounding the death, but have not yet opened a formal investigation.

A spokeswoman said: "Following the death of Sheku Bayoh at the Kirkcaldy Police Station on 5th May 2017, the Fife Police Service will be conducting an independent review of this matter.

"This will include checking all information available to us and working closely with the Family.

"During this review, the Fife Police Service will be liaising with the Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions to establish whether a criminal investigation will be conducted and if so, when this will take place."

One of the most popular features on our website yesterday was a picture gallery of revellers photographed over the years at popular Portsmouth nightclubs.

We were surprised to receive such a large response and we are pleased to announce that it is going to be extended to include all events with alcohol served in the future.

The pictures will be submitted to us directly by those who took them.

This will allow us to include new images, as well as showing some of the old favourites that have been so popular here over the years.

The gallery will be updated regularly, so if you are interested in taking part then please contact us.

We will also be doing a Q&A with some of the clubbers on Monday night, to make sure that we have asked as many questions as possible about what has been a fun time in Portsmouth over the years.

So if you will please email us with your details, we would like to hear from you now.

You can find out more about the Gallery on our website.

Thank you for your support of Portsmouth Live.

Today we examine the XFX Double Dissipation R9 280X video card, comparing it to a fiercely priced custom GeForce GTX 770. The result is an interesting comparison and gives us a better idea of what to expect from these cards when they were released.

While the GTX 670 was the benchmark card for the Radeon HD 7850, we wanted to find out if the same could be said for the R9 290X. With the R9 290X being more expensive than its predecessor and the GTX 780 Ti (and even the GTX 780) when it was released, it should have been expected that there would be a huge gap in price between them.

What we found was a clear gap in price: $200 versus $300. While this isn't great by any means, it's not like the R9 290X is going to be a bargain.

The R9 290X is a bit more expensive than most of its competitors. It costs a whopping $349.99 with a Founders Edition motherboard, and $439.99 without a board. It is also $100 more expensive than its predecessors.

At the time of writing, the R9 290X is in short supply, with few retailers still stocking the cards.

ZANESVILLE - The Zanesville Community Theatre will open its 56th season this weekend with the award-winning drama, "Of Mice and Men" by John Steinbeck.

The season opener is at 7:30 p.m. Wednesday, August 21, and the production continues through Sunday, August 25.

Tickets for "Of Mice and Men" are $21 for adults, $16 for seniors 65 and older and $10 for students.

"This is a wonderful American classic story about a group of young people in the Depression who go to work at a factory and are made to understand the dangers of working at a place where people are treated as disposable," said ZCT Executive Director Tom Hetrick. "The actors play very well together in a story with great dialogue."

"Of Mice and Men" was set between the years 1935 and 1940 and follows a group of young people who find themselves working in a mill and having no say in how their lives are going to be shaped.

The cast includes a number of familiar faces to the community who have been with the theater since its founding.

Diane Dierks will star as Edith Finch, a wife, mother, worker and friend from Zanesville.

Only two reports against members of the Jamaica Defence Force (JDF) were made to the Independent Commission of Investigations (INDECOM) last year, but JDF commanders say several other soldiers were disciplined or.

We hope these new developments will lead to a more serious investigation of the JDF and the allegations that it has committed serious crimes against civilians in the past.

The U.S. is the only country to have withdrawn troops from Liberia since the end of the country’s civil war in 2003. It sent roughly 2,300 troops to help train and assist the Liberian government, but withdrew them in 2011 after the African Union/International Community for Mission in Liberia (AUC) deployed troops to replace them.

The AUC mission was formed as a result of an agreement signed by Liberia, the United Nations and the United States in 2011. The mission was meant to enforce the peace deal and ensure elections held in 2014 were free and fair.

Liberia’s government said at the time its troops were withdrawn because of a series of deadly attacks by unidentified gunmen on civilian targets, including the United Nations mission in Monrovia.

On Tuesday, President George Weah said he had decided to order the withdrawal of U.S. troops from Liberia.

It was poignant sitting opposite the man whose ambition it had been to remake capitalism for an age of austerity. It was a reminder of how powerful the state is and how much it can change in a generation.

The Tories’ promise to “level up” Britain, as they called it, was always going to be a challenge, especially when you’re talking about the country’s most deprived areas. But it was also always going to require a different kind of party leader.

At the last general election, Theresa May was the closest thing we had to a socialist, and not just because she was a woman. She was also a left-wing socialist who had devoted her entire life to fighting inequality and defending the interests of working people.

She may have been a left-wing socialist who had spent her entire life fighting inequality and defending the interests of working people – but she was also a man. Her father, Stanley, was born in 1875 and spent the rest of his life working in steel mills.

Inequality and poverty are deeply entrenched in our society | Owen Jones Read more

That was a time when the male industrial worker was seen as the breadwinner who had to provide for their family and look after the home.

Heritage proponents in Southampton are upset that Bruce County intends to sell the 124-year-old former St. George’s Anglican Church at 815 Pine Street to a developer for development.

The property is currently listed at $1.9 million and has been on the market for about two years. The sale is contingent on a number of conditions, including obtaining a special use permit from the county planning department.

The former church was built in 1885, according to the listing.

On Friday morning, Heritage Southampton held a news conference outside the church, where they called for the project to be halted. They said the county needs to rezone the church to allow for commercial development at its current location.

“In order for an applicant to get a special use permit, they need to demonstrate how the use will enhance the character of the area,” says Susan Kuntz, heritage co-ordinator with Heritage Southampton. “We don’t know what the effect will be.”

Kuntz says that if the commercial development goes ahead, it could put a strain on the local economy.

“If you have a commercial activity in a historic building, it’s going to reduce the value of the building if there are no people coming into the community,” she says.

Israel Air Force is due to receive F-35 stealth fighters from manufacturer Lockheed Martin beginning in late 2015.

In the first phase of its procurement, Israel will purchase 40 F-35s, with the possibility of purchasing a further 100 aircraft.

The air force has also announced it will purchase 50 new jet trainers in addition to the Lockheed Martin F-35s.

Israeli Defense Minister Moshe Ya’alon announced the Israeli Air Force's purchase of new fighter jets from Lockheed Martin Monday during a visit to the company's plant in the US state of Texas.

"We have entered an agreement with Lockheed Martin for the purchase of four F-35A fighters and 50 F-16I fighters," Ya'alon said according to The Jerusalem Post. "The company is going to deliver these planes in 2014."

According to The Jerusalem Post, the deal was signed on November 12, 2013, at the Lockheed Martin facility in Fort Worth, Texas.

In addition, Ya'alon announced that Israel will also buy 50 new jet trainers made by Lockheed Martin.

"We have decided to acquire 50 planes of the Lockheed Martin F-16I trainer in addition to the four planes we are acquiring and another 50 planes of the F-35 fighter," Ya'alon said according to The Jerusalem Post.

The Peoria Rivermen came back to tie the game twice against the Rockford IceHogs but fell 4-2 before 5,521at the MetroCentre Friday night.

The Rivermen opened the scoring just 1:55 into the game when Andrew Ladd beat Rockford goalie Chad Johnson with a wrist shot through traffic.

Rockford answered at 16:22 of the first period when Chris Terry put one past the Peoria goaltender, who stopped the final eight shots of the period.

The Rivermen pulled even at 9:06 of the second period when Chris Kreider scored his 12th goal of the season.

The IceHogs regained the lead at 10:23 of the second period when Jeff Friesen scored his 28th goal of the season.

Rockford extended their lead to 3-1 four minutes into the third period when Zach Sanford scored his 20th goal of the season.

The Rivermen (17-20-8), who will play the Chicago Wolves Saturday night at the MetroCentre, are now 2-2-0 in their last five games.

Digital consumers are being bombarded with one kind of messaging or another. In the last year, I’ve been bombarded with several different messaging apps — all of them designed to give you the impression that they’re better than their competitors.

I’m not saying that every app is bad, or that every app is good. Apps like Facebook Messenger and Snapchat have huge user bases, and they have become part of people’s daily lives. But the sheer volume of new messaging apps has made it near impossible for users to get a handle on what’s good and bad.

Now we have Slack, which is a messaging app for businesses that’s been around for just over two years. The company has built a network of millions of developers and thousands of employees who are using Slack as an essential part of their daily work routine.

Slack’s most recent earnings report shows that its business messaging business alone grew over 70 percent from $1.7 million in revenue in 2016 to $3.6 million in 2017.

Warning shot to the City as trader Tom Hayes’ conviction over Libor scandal upheld | City A.M.
The City regulator has today confirmed that its appeal against the conviction of Tom Hayes will be heard by the Court of Appeal.
Hayes, a former Barclays trader, was convicted of two charges of conspiring to defraud Barclays and three of conspiring with a former colleague to manipulate Libor.
Read more: Barclays boss Jes Staley denies Libor manipulation allegations
He was cleared of one charge of conspiracy to defraud the bank, but guilty of two others, relating to his role in setting up a "Libor fixing" cartel within Barclays.
Hayes was convicted by a jury at the Old Bailey in November 2015.
As part of the conspiracy, Hayes and a fellow trader called Mark Allen used their trading positions to manipulate Libor to reduce their own Libor payments.
The pair had been put on trial alongside Barclays' former chief executive Bob Diamond, who admitted to an earlier conspiracy to fix Libor.
Hayes, now 47, is the first person to have been found guilty of conspiring to defraud a financial institution in relation to Libor.

Following Hurricane Sandy, members of the New York startup community have extended offers of help fellow entrepreneurs and other residents of the tri-state area caught without power and Internet by the storm.

The initiative has already been taken up by a number of companies in the area, including the likes of Zipcar, which has offered free rides to anyone with a car and Zipcar's parent company, Zagster, which is also offering discounted rentals to anyone who needs to travel for work. So far, more than 1,000 people have signed up for free rentals, according to Zagster CEO Chris Kohn.

"Everyone is pitching in," he said. "It's really incredible."

Zagster has also set up a GoFundMe page to raise money for those affected.

Zipcar is also offering discounts to those living in the affected areas.

"We have extended our special rates on all rentals through November 2nd for customers who need to travel for business, pleasure or both as a result of Hurricane Sandy," the company said on its website. "For the remainder of the month of October we will be offering 20% off for any ZipCar that is rented from November 1st through November 5th."

The clip was shot at LA's Echo Park neighborhood by a talented crew including cinematographer Ryan Osterberg, DP Chris Fenton and editor Chris Nix.

Bada$$'s "Temptations" video is being released on April 2 via GOOD Music. Listen to the track here, or watch the clip below.

The project follows his 2014 debut album, The Warm Up, which featured collaborations with Chance the Rapper, J Cole, and Wiz Khalifa, among others.

COGNAC, FRANCE — I have a lot of faith in my ability to taste and evaluate. But with the help of the French wine magazine Jour de Provence, I’m not sure that I could identify a single bottle in a crowded restaurant.

I can certainly tell you the names of the wines I tried, but I don’t think it was the same as tasting them myself.

The experience reminded me of when I was a kid, trying to follow the directions from the cookbook “The Joy of Cooking” or playing the piano in the dark. It’s easy to get disoriented and lost.

I’m not entirely convinced that this is a good thing.

Tottenham Hotspur striker Son Heung-min was overwhelmingly chosen as the athlete of the year among Korean sportspeople.

The 27-year-old scored the winner against Manchester City in Sunday's Champions League final in Kiev, becoming the first South Korean to win the coveted trophy.

Son took home a trophy that had belonged to his father, an Olympic gold medallist in taekwondo.

In addition to the award, Son will receive £50,000, a silver medal and a pair of tickets for the 2018 FIFA World Cup finals in Russia.

The Tottenham talisman scored three goals in four finals appearances to win the Champions League for the first time in his career, having previously played for Bayer Leverkusen and Southampton.

Son has been selected by a panel of sports journalists and experts alongside basketball star LeBron James, England's rugby sevens players and baseball's Justin Verlander.

Son Heung-min, 29, became the first South Korean to win the Champions League (Martin Rickett/PA)

Former Spurs midfielder Jason McAteer, who won the award in 2010, said: "To be a finalist in this category, it's unheard of.

In the pantheon of Santa Barbara artists, perhaps no star shines brighter than Ray Strong (1905-2006). His work is so striking and unique that it can be difficult to determine where to start. His unique style is based on the use of natural light and black and white photography. He is best known for his large scale paintings of Santa Barbara Coast locations, including the Santa Barbara Zoo, the historic Buellton Inn, and the Old Santa Barbara Cemetery.

Strong was born and raised in Santa Barbara, California. He attended San Marcos High School, then transferred to Cal Poly Pomona where he studied art. He was an active member of the local theater community from an early age. He began painting full-time as a teenager. He exhibited his work in various galleries around town and was fortunate enough to win several awards. He was also an active member of The Artists' League where he shared his talent with fellow art students.

Strong married Marion Albers in 1947. They had three children, two sons and one daughter. Marion passed away in 1979. Strong continued to paint, but his work was done in an entirely different manner.

The Facebook data privacy and Russian election interference scandals show that artificial intelligence is still not up to many critical jobs in the technology sector. But that doesn’t mean it’s a bad thing.

To be sure, there are some areas of AI that are particularly suited for automation or are better suited to machine learning than others. This is likely because a particular AI algorithm will be better for a certain task at an organization than for another, if only for the reason that the algorithms are optimized for different tasks.

Yet, even in these case studies, the technologies are not all equally useful. For example, the use of machine learning in the fields of social media analytics and mobile marketing can provide insights into what works, but it cannot help organizations decide which marketing techniques to deploy.

A recent study by Google’s DeepMind AI division showed that machines can perform nearly as well as humans in identifying patterns in news content, but they are only useful when those patterns occur regularly, in a predictable manner, and in large quantities.

“We’ve already seen advances in understanding things like the weather,” DeepMind’s co-founder, Demis Hassabis, told me in an interview.

The 3-year-old boy squirmed in his mother’s arms, an IV drip unspooling from his wrist, his skin flush with a pale pink rash.

Her name is Maima, and she lives in Lebanon. Her husband was killed on the same day his wife gave birth to their son, a baby boy who would be named Mohammed.

His father and brother were among the dozens of people killed in the suicide bombing that leveled a large section of the city’s downtown area on Aug. 6, 2016. The Islamic State group claimed responsibility for the attack, which left at least 250 people wounded.

“I saw my husband’s body in the morgue,” Maima told The Associated Press. “He was crying and saying he was sorry. He was saying he didn’t want me to leave him.”

Mohammed was one of nine children, all born to Maima and her husband, a local journalist.

All had names that began with “M.”

“We have no names or surname. We just call Mohammed ‘Mohammed,’ ” she said.

The last few years have proved challenging for offshore drilling major Transocean, which saw its day rates and contracting activity post significant declines. In the second quarter of 2017, the company’s day rates were down 33% year-over-year, while its contract backlog was down by 22%.

As the company looks to stabilize its business, President and CEO Stephen Schwarzman has taken a number of actions, including the sale of several assets and divestitures of non-core positions to focus on its core business. But with a large amount of debt, Transocean still has a long way to go before it can fully shed its debt burden.

Transocean’s debt is currently at $3.4 billion, and while this is not an unprecedented amount for a large maritime company, it is significant nonetheless and represents a significant burden for the company.

While Transocean’s high debt level puts pressure on its balance sheet, it also provides the company with a significant amount of flexibility to make necessary changes.

A Look at Transocean’s Debt

Source: Transocean

As of June 30, 2017, the company had $3.4 billion in total debt outstanding.

A study led by researcher Lisa Kellogg of William & Mary's Virginia Institute of Marine Science shows that a restored oyster reef can remove up to 10 times more nitrogen from Chesapeake.

The team used a newly-developed technology called 'Nitrogen Cleaning Technology' (NCT). The technology involves a process known as 'bleaching,' which removes nutrients from the water column and turns them into a harmless fertilizer.

"The results are very promising," Kellogg said. "It's exciting."

The research was published in the Journal of Applied Ecology on March 15, 2014.

The Virginia Institute of Marine Science is an independent, nonprofit research organization that conducts pioneering marine science research.

Some 190 Nigerian schoolgirls remain missing after being abducted last week, their head teacher has told the BBC - far more than the official figure.

"I don't think that there is any hope for this group of girls because we are still waiting for the confirmation from the government and the authorities," said Mohammed Abubakar.

The authorities had set a deadline of Monday to release the girls or face an international condemnation of Nigeria's failure to protect them.

The Nigerian government insists that it is doing all it can to find the girls.

The abduction took place on Friday in Chibok, a town in Borno state, and was recorded on mobile phone footage.

It involved about 300 people, including community members and militants, who seized the girls at gunpoint.

Mr Abubakar said that some 200 people had been released, and some others were being held for ransom.

He said he had not been able to contact many of the students since they went missing.

"Every day I am calling them, but so far, no one has picked up by phone," he said. "

Rabiah Hutchinson is used to being talked about.

For the past three years, she's been featured on the website of the University of Iowa’s Health Sciences Center as part of a study about women’s self-image and self-worth in America.

Hutchinson, 25, an aspiring doctor, has been studying the self-esteem of young women for nearly two decades.

Hutchinson was one of 12 students who took part in an anonymous survey about their self image. The study looked at how women’s self-esteem changes from one year to the next, how they feel about themselves generally and how they define themselves.

"I think it definitely affects what you see yourself as," Hutchinson said. "It's hard to know where that comes from. I'm just trying to find out."

When the University of Iowa Health Sciences Center released the findings of its study to the public last week, it included a disclaimer: "The opinions expressed here do not necessarily reflect those of the University of Iowa.”

"As you might imagine, this was an interesting project," said Dr. Laura O'Connell, associate professor of psychology and co-director of the university's Women's Health Research Center. "

Former President Bill Clinton speaks during a campaign stop for his wife Democratic presidential candidate Hilary Clinton, Jan. 25, 2016 in New Hampshire.

NEW HAMPSHIRE — Former President Bill Clinton on Monday urged voters to reject Donald Trump's "divisive and racist" rhetoric, saying the Republican nominee is a danger to the country.

Clinton said there are many things Trump has said that he himself would never say or do but that he sees as dangerous.

"If you're looking for someone to get behind, someone who will support your values, a proven leader who will fight for working people, someone who will stand up to this president, then look no further," Clinton said at a rally in Manchester.

Clinton was campaigning for her wife, Hillary Clinton, less than two weeks before the Nov. 8 election that could determine control of Congress. She is trying to turn the race against Trump into a referendum on her opponent, while Trump has been trying to turn the focus back to her.

She said Trump's comments on immigrants in general and Muslims in particular are "dangerous."

A number of Warriors players actively recruited DeMarcus Cousins to the Bay Area, but the newly-signed Warrior can definitely state which one of them presented the worst pitch.

Cousins was joined by his longtime agent, Rich Paul, and a trio of Warriors assistant coaches, including assistant coach and former player Keith Smart.

“I brought Keith with me because I wanted him to recruit me,” Cousins said, according to Shams Charania of The Athletic. “Rich Paul was with me. He’s been with me since I got drafted.”

Paul is one of the main agents who represents the likes of LeBron James, Kawhi Leonard, and Paul George. Cousins, meanwhile, is a free agent after the Warriors traded him to the Pelicans in August.

“I was going to go to him,” Cousins said. “I was going to go to a lot of guys. All of them tried to recruit me. Everyone in the NBA tried to recruit me. I knew it was going to be tough.”

While Cousins didn’t get the best of the pitches, he did come up with a solid reason to join the Warriors.

“They were all telling me things that I needed to hear to make me want to come here,” Cousins said.

Although the laboriously negotiated and long-delayed Middle East "road map" received a diplomatic boost by the recent intervention of George W. Bush, it is still far from clear whether the United States will be able to get a broad consensus on Arab-Israeli relations in the time remaining before the second anniversary of the second Israeli war against Lebanon.

The road map was reached in Cairo at the end of September and agreed upon in a series of conferences in Washington and Jerusalem between September 15 and 26. The road map was preceded by the first summit meeting of the Arab League in Cairo and then by the first meeting of the Quartet of Middle East mediators, led by American Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice, which took place in Washington on October 13 and 14.

With the US support, the Arab League endorsed the road map and all Arab states have expressed their full support for it.

But while the Arab League has supported the road map, the Quartet, which includes the United Nations, the European Union and the United States, remains divided over its implementation.

The United States, which is the primary supporter of the road map, has been pushing for a "road map for peace," in which the Palestinians would give up their demands for an independent state with east Jerusalem as its capital and Israel would give up its claims to historic lands.

Minnesota State High School League has approved concussion insurance for student athletes starting this fall.

The $10 million policy will cover medical costs and lost wages from concussions, mild traumatic brain injuries and other injuries.

More than a dozen states already have such policies in place.

The Minnesota State High School League voted unanimously Wednesday to approve the new insurance plan. The plan goes into effect Aug. 1.

"I think the important thing is that it's a way to help kids, to get their health covered," said Minnesota High School League executive director Tom Hjelmgaard. "We know that kids are injured all the time, and it costs us money."

The Minnesota law will make all student-athletes in Minnesota eligible for coverage starting in the fall of 2014.

"That's a great day for the Minnesota State High School League," Hjelmgaard said. "This is something that we've been working on for many years."

Hjelmgaard said the league's board has debated the issue for many years and finally decided to go ahead with the change.

The Patently-O blog this week took a look at how the number of pending patent cases is changing.

The blog post titled “The number of pending patent cases has increased for the fourth straight year” looked specifically at the number of cases that have been filed in the United States and found that the number of pending patent cases has increased for four straight years.

The post points out that patents are now being filed at a rate of about one new patent every two weeks. In fact, patent filings have increased by approximately 60 percent over the last 20 years.

In addition to those who are filing patents, there is a steady flow of patent attorneys filling out patent applications. The blogs also noted that patent attorneys are filing more patent applications than ever before.

The data from the USPTO revealed that the number of patent applications filed as of July 2013 was up compared to July 2012. There were 9,869 patent applications filed that month, which was an increase of 3,937 patent applications filed during the same period last year.

For any patent attorney – or anyone who wants to be better prepared for the ever-increasing number of patent applications flooding the patent office – the USPTO has released a useful resource to help you understand the process.

The Seven Cs of Political Leadership!

By

I’m not a big fan of “seven Cs” – “co-operation, compromise, consensus, communication, consultation, collegiality and candor,” as the late Ted Kennedy famously said. I like the idea of “five Cs” better: “courage, conviction, character, commitment and compassion.” But you get what you deserve when you vote for Democrats, because that’s what they’re really all about.

And so it is with this column. I hope to do my part in helping to shape our country toward greatness. I can help by focusing on those seven Cs that will make that happen over the next two years, and beyond.

First, we need to work together to build a positive vision for America. It’s hard enough to focus on the bad stuff these days. We’ve got a president who seems to relish saying things like, “We’re going to lose our country if we don’t fix this.” The other day, he said, “I think the problem with the Republican Party is we don’t talk about the jobs.” The Democrats should be talking about jobs, too, but they’re not.

And so it is with this column . I hope to do my part in helping to shape our country toward greatness . I can assist by concentrating on those seven Cs that will contribute to achieving that over the next two years and further. Initially, we must collaborate to create an uplifting vision for America. It's already challenging to concentrate on the negative aspects these days. President Trump's administration has been more concerned about climate change than any other American president, according to a new study.

The Washington Post published the report Tuesday, which found that the Trump White House has issued nine executive orders since January 2017 that have specifically addressed climate change.

"President Trump has taken an unusually aggressive stance on climate change, and this has been reflected in many of his actions," the study stated. "The number of executive orders issued by the Trump administration on climate change is more than twice the number issued by his predecessor, Barack Obama."

The Post analyzed White House data on the number of executive orders related to climate change between January 2017 and January 2018 and found that Trump's team issued nine executive orders on the issue.

When it comes to safety in the workplace, organizational culture can be quite literally a matter of life and death. While we can’t all afford our own medical insurance, or choose to forgo paid leave, we can make our employers more aware of the importance of providing safe workplaces without breaking the bank.

The most effective way to do that is by developing an employee culture where employees feel empowered to speak up and share their concerns. This may sound like common sense, but many organizations have a hard time making the time-consuming commitment to developing a culture of workplace wellbeing.

How should your organization approach this task?

To develop an effective culture of workplace wellbeing, you need to first establish goals for your employees and put in place a strategy to ensure that they achieve those goals. While some individuals may feel that their current job is enough, a workforce with the right goals and the proper tools can make a significant difference in the overall health of your workplace.

Goal setting

Employees who are passionate about workplace wellbeing and are motivated to do their part to improve the environment around them will be much more likely to take steps to improve the environment than those who are satisfied with where they are.

New Jersey lawmakers approved a plan to reinstate the individual mandate to try to stabilize the health insurance market.

The state House of Representatives voted 98-0 on Tuesday to repeal the law, which required people to have health insurance or face a penalty.

ADVERTISEMENT

New Jersey Republicans have argued the mandate was an unconstitutional tax and would have forced people into the individual insurance market anyway.

But Democrats said the repeal was the only way to force insurance companies to sell policies to people who didn’t qualify for subsidies.

“This is not about getting rid of the president of the United States, it’s about getting rid of a law that is no longer valid,” said Democratic Rep. Bonnie Watson Coleman.

Republicans said the health care law, known as Obamacare, was too costly for taxpayers and that people were able to get coverage through their employers.

“When you take away that penalty, we’re not going to see the same kind of increases in premiums that we did previously,” said Republican Rep. Thomas MacArthur.

House Speaker Vincent Prieto (D-Hudson) announced last week that the state would drop its lawsuit against the Affordable Care Act.

Luis Suarez was distraught following Liverpool’s draw with Crystal Palace on Monday, but the Uruguayan can take a measure of solace in the fact that he made history.

Liverpool’s all-time leading goalscorer scored his 200th La Liga goal as his side claimed a 1-1 draw against a Palace side who were playing in their first home Premier League game since March.

Suarez took his total to 201 goals for the Reds, breaking the record set by Kenny Dalglish in the 1985-86 season.

The striker also became the highest scorer in the Premier League era.

And the former Barcelona and Atletico Madrid man admitted to being emotional after scoring his milestone goal, but insisted that Liverpool are capable of winning trophies this season.

“I didn’t expect it,” said Suarez. “It was an unbelievable feeling, but I’m very happy about it.

“Of course I was emotional. I cried when I achieved it, but it is a very good feeling to help my team-mates.

“We have the strength to win the title. We are playing fantastic football.

Authorities in Bexar County, Texas, on Thursday responded to reports of three individuals with fatal gunshot wounds inside a gated community.

Bexar County Sheriff Javier Salazar said deputies were called to the community of San Antonio Ranchos at about 2:30 p.m. for a report of "shots fired." When they arrived, they found three people wounded in the community's gated community.

Police said the three people died of their injuries.

Salazar said the three victims were believed to be inside their homes when the shooting took place.

"There's a lot of surveillance cameras in that area so we don't really know exactly where these people were but we do have reason to believe that there was some type of argument or altercation inside the home and then the shots were fired," Salazar said.

The circumstances leading up to the shooting are not known.

Salazar said the names of the deceased will be released by the Bexar County medical examiner's office after their families have been notified.

"As soon as the family members of the deceased are notified, all information will be released.

The conference not only produces some of the best basketball players in the world, but it also produces some of the most consistent teams.
